name: xAI Grok API
what_it_does: Provides access to xAI's Grok family of large language models (Grok-1, Grok-2, Grok-Vision) via an OpenAI-compatible API, with a unique differentiator of real-time access to X/Twitter data for up-to-date world knowledge.
use_cases: Build news-aware agents that query Grok for real-time social media trends and breaking news synthesis via its X/Twitter data access
Generate and analyze images using Grok-Vision for multimodal document processing or visual Q&A pipelines
Drop Grok models into existing OpenAI-compatible agent frameworks for LLM provider diversification with minimal code changes
Power research agents that need current event awareness beyond a static knowledge cutoff date
Create social media content agents that leverage Grok's understanding of X platform conventions and trending topics
not_for: Production workloads requiring guaranteed SLAs — xAI API is still in early/beta access with limited enterprise commitments
Regulated industries requiring SOC2 or HIPAA compliance certifications that xAI has not yet published
Cost-sensitive high-volume inference where cheaper alternatives like DeepSeek or Mistral are sufficient
best_when: Best when an agent specifically needs access to real-time information from X/Twitter or requires a large capable model with OpenAI API compatibility and tolerance for an early-stage provider.
avoid_when: Avoid when the workload demands enterprise-grade SLAs, compliance certifications, or predictable rate limits that a beta-stage API cannot reliably provide.

auth.notes: API key passed as Bearer token, identical pattern to OpenAI. Keys provisioned via the xAI console at console.x.ai. No per-key scoping or RBAC available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Early access credits provided during beta period; amount varies and may change as the API matures
pricing.paid_tiers: Grok-2: ~$2/1M input tokens, ~$10/1M output tokens; Grok-Vision pricing similar; rates subject to change as API exits beta
pricing.notes: Pricing is competitive with GPT-4 class models. Real-time X data access may carry additional costs as the offering matures. Beta pricing may not reflect GA pricing.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits are not fully published during beta; early access accounts report limits in the range of 60 requests/minute with per-hour token caps

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses mirror OpenAI format (type, message, code) but documentation on specific error codes is sparse for a beta product.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Agents should implement their own deduplication and must handle partial failures from the beta infrastructure.
known_agent_gotchas: Real-time X/Twitter data access behavior is not fully documented — agents cannot reliably predict when or whether live data will influence a response
API is in beta with no published SLA, meaning outages and breaking changes can occur without advance notice
OpenAI compatibility is not 100% — some advanced features like fine-tuning, assistants, and batch APIs are not available
Rate limits are opaque during beta and can change without warning, making capacity planning for agent fleets unreliable
No function/tool calling support parity with GPT-4 Turbo — agents relying on structured tool use may need fallback logic

security_score_components.security_notes: No published security audits or compliance certifications as of early access. TLS enforced. Data handling practices for real-time X data ingestion are not fully disclosed.
